story: Brrr! Teeth chattering, icicles forming, Seminole, Texas experienced its coldest day ever in 1933. On February 8, the temperature plummeted to an unbelievable -23°F (-31°C). This bone-chilling cold set a new state record. Imagine frozen eyelashes and steaming breath in the usually warm Lone Star State!

title: The World Wide Web's Big Day
story: Computers around the globe buzzed with excitement on February 8, 1996. Thousands of people joined forces for '24 Hours in Cyberspace', a huge Internet project. Photographers, writers, and web designers worked non-stop to capture life online. They created a massive digital snapshot of how the Internet was changing our world. It was like a giant online scrapbook!

title: Giant Plane Takes to the Skies
story: A massive new cargo plane spread its wings and soared into the air for the first time. The Boeing 747-8 Freighter, a huge aircraft designed to carry tons of goods around the world, took off from Paine Field, Washington on February 8, 2010. This maiden flight marked an exciting moment in aviation history, as the newest member of the famous 747 family showed off its impressive size and power.

title: TV Crosses the Ocean
story: A fuzzy face flickered to life on a screen in Hartsdale, New York. It was the first TV image to travel across the Atlantic Ocean! On February 8, 1928, this groundbreaking broadcast marked a huge leap for communication technology. Though blurry by today's standards, it showed the incredible potential of television to connect people across vast distances.

title: Boeing's Metal Marvel Takes Flight
story: Zoom! A sleek, shiny airplane roared down the runway. The Boeing 247 wasn't just any plane - it was the first all-metal passenger aircraft. On February 8, 1933, this flying machine took to the skies, changing air travel forever. Its strong metal body made it safer and faster than older wooden planes.
activity_question: What was special about the Boeing 247?

It was the largest plane ever built
It could fly to the moon
activity_answer: It was the first all-metal passenger aircraft

title: A Pen-tastic Invention Takes Shape
story: Tired of messy ink blots? Louis Waterman felt the same way. On February 8, 1883, he started experimenting to create something better. His goal? A pen that wouldn't leak! Waterman's tinkering led to the fountain pen, a writing tool that carries its own ink supply. His invention changed how people write, making it easier and cleaner for everyone.

title: A Beacon of Learning in the Netherlands
story: In 1575, a new light of knowledge shone in the Netherlands. William I of Orange founded the University of Leiden, giving it a powerful motto: 'Praesidium Libertatis'. This Latin phrase means 'Bastion of Liberty'. On February 8, the university opened its doors, becoming the oldest in the country. It still stands today as a symbol of academic freedom and excellence.
